summary: Great soundboard recording with minor tape‑hiss, highlighted by a shredding Fire on the Mountain and stellar Black Peter
review: The remastered soundboard capture is clear and generally excellent, though a faint generational hiss can be heard in quieter moments. Musically the show shines – Fire on the Mountain “shreds,” Black Peter ranks among the best versions, and Space turns into an extended jam, all backed by tight post‑drum work.

ai_rating.rationale: Four substantive reviews rate the show 4–5 stars and praise both performance and recording; the weighted average yields a solid 4.2 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered soundboard with clear mix that improves through set 1; minor tape‑generation hiss audible in quiet passages but overall great recording quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 mix builds nicely, and the second half delivers jam‑heavy moments (Space) and strong post‑drum sections; overall pacing is smooth and engaging.

band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers praised for the powerful post‑drums section and tight playing throughout.
